reproductiveEndocrineContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Hormone Health is currently a reproductive/endocrine boundary package. It can document what evidence is available and what is missing, but it cannot produce carrier, fertility, hormone, pregnancy, lab, treatment, testing, or reproductive-action conclusions from local evidence alone.

reproductiveEndocrineContext.professionalReview: Use this output only as preparation for a professional conversation. Health or reproductive decisions require qualified review with clinical history, symptoms, medications, labs, family history, partner context, pregnancy context when relevant, and validated testing scope.

professionalReview: Do not use this local fixture to change medication, supplements, diet, testing, screening, procedures, fertility treatment, pregnancy plans, or reproductive decisions.

Bring current symptoms, medical and family history, medications, labs, prior testing, partner context, pregnancy context when relevant, and ancestry context to a qualified professional if discussing this topic.
Treat unavailable model rows as unavailable, not as normal, abnormal, low risk, high risk, reassuring, diagnostic, actionable, or all clear.
